description: Lap 1 corrects Miranda's page but leaves the fabricated Thompson authority, so the independent gate still fails. Lap 2 removes Thompson and the gate passes. This makes convergence visible with zero API keys and zero external tools.

interactions.agent_output: Correcting Miranda's page from 999 to 436. I believe the remaining authorities are valid.

actions.content:
# Brief in Support of Motion to Suppress

Plaintiff respectfully submits the following authorities in support of the motion.

The right to appointed counsel is fundamental to a fair trial. See Gideon v.
Wainwright, 372 U.S. 335 (1963).

Evidence obtained through an unlawful search must be excluded from trial. See
Mapp v. Ohio, 367 U.S. 643 (1961).

A suspect subjected to custodial interrogation must first be warned of the right
to remain silent. See Miranda v. Arizona, 384 U.S. 436 (1966).

The Court has since recognized a broader constitutional privacy interest in this
context. See Thompson v. Halden, 599 U.S. 1201 (2021).

Segregation in public education violates the Equal Protection Clause. See Brown
v. Board of Education, 347 U.S. 483 (1954).

interactions.notes: The agent claims done, but the gate catches the remaining fabricated authority and forces another lap.

interactions.agent_output: The gate still rejects 599 U.S. 1201. Thompson v. Halden is fabricated, so I am removing that authority without changing the reporter or checker.

interactions.notes: The second correction makes the independent citation gate pass.

interactions.agent_output: The gate has passed; no further changes are needed.
